touches.ai_summary: Cold call attempt to Santiago Green, VP of Sales at RiverVentures. No answer, left voicemail introducing AskElephant's call recording and coaching platform.
Second cold call attempt to Santiago Green. No answer, no voicemail left this time.
LinkedIn connection request sent to Santiago Green with brief message about helping eCommerce sales teams improve coaching through call intelligence.
Cold email sent to Santiago highlighting how AskElephant helps sales leaders in eCommerce scale coaching with AI-powered call analysis. No response received.
Follow-up email sent with case study of similar-sized eCommerce company. Santiago responded expressing interest in learning more about call recording capabilities.

questions_asked.question: Do you integrate with Salesforce?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we have native Salesforce integration for automatic call logging and syncing.

touches.ai_summary: Qualification call with Santiago Green. Discussed current state: 8-person sales team struggling with inconsistent coaching. Santiago mentioned they're early in evaluation and also looking at Gong. Identified need for call recording, AI summaries, and coaching workflows.

questions_asked.question: What's driving you to look at conversation intelligence now?
questions_asked.answer: We're scaling the team from 8 to 12 reps this quarter and I can't listen to enough calls to coach everyone effectively.

questions_asked.question: How does your pricing compare to Gong?
questions_asked.answer: We're typically 30-40% more cost-effective for teams under 20 reps, with comparable feature sets for core use cases.

touches.ai_summary: Email sent with discovery questionnaire about current sales process, tech stack, and coaching workflow. No response yet.
Check-in call attempt to Santiago. No answer, left voicemail asking if he had questions about the discovery questionnaire.
Follow-up email asking if there's a better time to connect and offering to answer any questions before the next scheduled call.
Another call attempt to Santiago. No answer, no voicemail left.

Follow-up discovery call with Santiago. Discussed pain points: reps not following discovery framework, losing deals to better-prepared competitors. Santiago confirmed budget is available but needs to make the case internally. Mentioned they haven't brought in leadership yet.

questions_asked.question: Who else should be involved in evaluating this?
questions_asked.answer: Probably just me for now—I own the sales tools budget. I'll loop in the CRO later if needed.

questions_asked.question: Can we start with a smaller pilot?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we can do a pilot with a subset of users, but our pricing is actually more efficient at the 8-seat level you mentioned.

touches.ai_summary: Product demo with Santiago covering call recording, AI summarization, and coaching workflows. Santiago engaged throughout, asked detailed questions about automation capabilities. He mentioned Gong demo is scheduled for next week and wants to compare before moving forward.

questions_asked.question: How does the unlimited automation tier work?
questions_asked.answer: You get unlimited AI summaries, automated follow-up suggestions, and custom coaching scorecards without per-call fees.

questions_asked.question: What's the implementation timeline?
questions_asked.answer: Typically 1-2 weeks for full deployment including integrations and user training.

questions_asked.question: What's your timeline for making a decision?
questions_asked.answer: Ideally next month—I want this in place before we onboard the new reps in May.

objections: Need to see Gong's offering before committing

touches.ai_summary: Proposal review call with Santiago. Presented 8-seat unlimited automation plan at $8,900 ACV. Santiago pushed back on price, mentioned Gong came in lower. Discussed ROI and agreed to explore discount options to match his budget constraints.

questions_asked.question: Can you get closer to $5,000 annually?
questions_asked.answer: That's a significant reduction, but let me see what flexibility I have given your timeline and commitment to the unlimited tier.

questions_asked.question: How did the Gong demo compare?
questions_asked.answer: They have more enterprise features we don't need. Your automation capabilities actually fit better, but their pricing was more competitive.

questions_asked.question: If we can make the numbers work, can we move quickly?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, if we align on pricing I can get you a contract by end of week.

objections: Price is higher than competitive option

touches.ai_summary: Email sent with revised proposal at $5,100 ACV (42.6% discount) to match budget. Santiago responded positively, said this works and asked about contract terms and start date.

questions_asked.question: Is this an annual contract?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, annual commitment with the option to expand seats mid-contract at the same per-seat rate.

touches.ai_summary: Pricing and terms finalization call with Santiago. Confirmed $5,100 ACV, 8 seats, unlimited automation tier, annual contract. Discussed implementation timeline starting early May. Santiago confirmed he can sign without additional approvals.

questions_asked.question: Do you need to run this by your CRO or finance?
questions_asked.answer: No, this is within my approval limit. I'll just give them a heads-up once it's signed.

questions_asked.question: When can we kick off implementation?
questions_asked.answer: We can start onboarding as soon as the contract is executed—targeting first week of May.

questions_asked.question: Any other stakeholders who should be looped in before we finalize?
questions_asked.answer: No, I'm good to move forward. Send over the contract and I'll get it signed this week.
